seoTitle: How to Build a Gantt Chart in Power BI (Timeline Guide)
metaDescription: Follow this guide to build a Gantt chart in Power BI using timeline visuals, ideal for tracking project schedules and milestones.
intro: Project timelines are much easier to manage when they're visualized rather than buried in a spreadsheet. This guide walks through building a Gantt chart in Power BI using timeline visuals, so you can track tasks, milestones, and schedules at a glance.

steps.heading: Set up Power BI and your data source
steps.body: Load your project data into Power BI, making sure you have task names, start dates, and end dates or durations.

steps.heading: Add your data to the Gantt visual
steps.body: Bring in a Gantt-style timeline visual and map your task, start date, and duration fields to it.

steps.heading: Create the Gantt chart
steps.body: Configure the visual so tasks appear as bars along a timeline, reflecting each task's schedule.

steps.heading: Customize your visuals
steps.body: Adjust colors, groupings, and labels so the chart clearly shows task owners, phases, or milestones.

steps.heading: Apply it to project tracking
steps.body: Use the finished Gantt chart for scenarios like marketing campaign timelines or product development phases.

faq.question: Do I need a custom visual to build a Gantt chart in Power BI?
faq.answer: Yes, Power BI doesn't include a native Gantt chart, so you'll typically add one from the visuals gallery.

faq.question: Can the Gantt chart update automatically as project data changes?
faq.answer: Yes, since it's built from your data source, the chart refreshes automatically when the underlying data updates.

faq.question: Can I show task dependencies on the Gantt chart?
faq.answer: Depending on the visual you use, some support showing links between related tasks, though this varies by visual.

faq.question: Is this suitable for tracking multiple projects at once?
faq.answer: Yes, by adding a project or category field, you can filter or group the Gantt chart to cover several projects.
